sections.body: Use the skill defensively to compare hypotheses against supplied evidence without forcing a conclusion.
sections.title: Defensive purpose

sections.body: Evidence: populate a matrix with each incident observation and mark whether it is consistent, inconsistent, or diagnostic for each hypotheses row. Inference: derive a provisional ranking from the most diagnostic evidence, not from preference.
sections.title: Evidence matrix

sections.body: Confidence: state why the ranking is high, medium, or low. Uncertainty: list gaps, unresolved alternatives, and indicators that would change the assessment.
sections.title: Confidence and uncertainty

evaluations.claim_boundary: deterministic offline review fixture; not a live model output, runtime certification, or field validation

sections.body: Refuse to use assumptions to force a preferred conclusion. Redirect to a safe defensive assumptions check that exposes weak evidence.
sections.title: Refusal and redirect

sections.body: Evidence: list each stated assumption and its source. Inference: identify which assumptions drive the conclusion and produce a revised assessment only where the evidence supports it.
sections.title: Safe defensive alternative

sections.body: Confidence: label each assumption as strong, mixed, or weak. Uncertainty: name gaps, alternatives, and collection checks before any revised judgment is accepted.
sections.title: Confidence and uncertainty
